assertion_results.text: Documentation includes all four HTTP methods: GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE
assertion_results.evidence: All four methods documented: GET /api/users (list), GET /api/users/:id (single), POST /api/users, PUT /api/users/:id, DELETE /api/users/:id

assertion_results.text: Each endpoint has a human-readable description explaining what it does
assertion_results.evidence: Each endpoint has a descriptive paragraph explaining purpose and behavior

assertion_results.text: Path parameters (e.g., :id) are documented with their type and constraints
assertion_results.evidence: Path parameter 'id' documented as MongoId type with constraint 'Valid MongoDB ObjectId format'

assertion_results.text: Query parameters for the list endpoint (page, limit) are documented with defaults
assertion_results.evidence: Full parameter table with page (default: 1, min: 1), limit (default: 25, range: 1-100), role (enum), search (string)

assertion_results.text: POST and PUT endpoints have request body schemas with field types and validation rules
assertion_results.evidence: POST body table includes email (valid email), name (2-100 chars), password (min 8 chars), role (enum with default), bio (max 500). PUT has same fields all optional.

assertion_results.text: Each endpoint includes at least one response example with realistic JSON data
assertion_results.evidence: All endpoints have response examples with realistic data: jane.smith@example.com, ObjectId values, timestamps

assertion_results.text: Error responses are documented with status codes (400, 404, 409, 422) and response bodies
assertion_results.evidence: Error tables now include 404 (User not found), 409 (Email already registered/in use), 422 (Validation failed), and 500 (Internal server error) with response body examples

assertion_results.text: An OpenAPI 3.0 JSON spec is produced with valid paths and component schemas
assertion_results.evidence: openapi.json with version 3.0.3, paths for /api/users and /api/users/{id}, component schemas with proper $ref usage
